RepresentativeInfo.CapitolAddress: MO House of Representatives, 201 West Capitol Avenue, Room 102-BB, Jefferson City MO 65101
RepresentativeInfo.Biography:
Representative Pattie Mansur, a Democrat, proudly represents part of Jackson County (District 25) in the Missouri House of Representatives. She was elected to her first two-year term in November 2024.
Mansur graduated from the University of Missouri, where she earned her Bachelor of Journalism and a Master of Business Administration from the University of Missouri in Kansas City. With over 30 years of experience in the non-profit sector in the Kansas City area, and as a current program manager in philanthropy-focused health disparities, she has developed and brings a deep understanding of health policy, communications, and public relations.
In addition to her legislative duties, Mansur currently serves on the board of directors for Girls on the Run of Greater Kansas City, has served on the Kansas City School Board, and is a member of the Greater Kansas City Women’s Political Caucus.
Mansur values family life and enjoys spending time with her husband, Michael, their three children, and two grandchildren.

SponsoredBill.LongTitle: Modifies provisions relating to telehealth services
Authorizes a state sales tax exemption for the purchase of diapers, incontinence products, and feminine hygiene products
Specifies that the department of health and senior services shall not deny trauma center status to a hospital based on its distance from another trauma center
Modifies provisions relating to child protection
Modifies provisions relating to MO HealthNet benefits

CoSponsoredBill.LongTitle: Requires charter schools to obtain a certificate of need issued by the state board of education in order to be eligible to operate
Requires health care facilities to post a sign warning of the potential criminal consequences of assaulting health care professionals
Designates the last week in April each year as "Infertility Awareness Week"
